groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Topic, audience, goal, key points, language, length, tone, and publishing identity.

groups.title: Editorial Direction
groups.description: Angle, knowledge level, voice, narrative approach, and exclusions.

groups.title: SEO & Discovery
groups.description: Keywords, intent, location, links, and snippet opportunities.

groups.title: Facts, Sources & Restrictions
groups.description: Evidence, quotes, claim handling, privacy, jurisdiction, disclosures.

groups.title: Output & Conversion
groups.description: Headlines, FAQ, CTA, destination, and formatting.

variables.label: Article topic
variables.hint: State the main subject in one clear sentence.

help.why: Defines the article’s central subject.
help.example: How small teams write honest product copy without fake social proof
help.avoid: Several unrelated topics in one article.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.hint: Select the single most important outcome.

help.why: Changes the article’s emphasis, depth, and conversion logic.
help.avoid: Stacking rank + sell + lead gen as equal goals.

variables.label: Article format
variables.hint: Select the structure that best fits the topic.

help.why: Determines the platform-native structure of the article.
help.avoid: Picking a format that fights the topic (e.g. list for a pure argument).

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Who will read this, and what matters to them?
variables.hint: Describe the intended readers, context, and main needs.

help.why: Controls relevance, vocabulary, examples, and explanation depth.
help.example: Founders and content leads at early-stage B2B products
help.avoid: Assuming every reader is already an expert or a buyer.

variables.label: Reader outcome
variables.placeholder: What should readers understand, decide, or do afterward?
variables.hint: Describe the practical result the article should create.

help.why: Keeps the article focused on a useful reader result.
help.example: They can write one clearer product update without inventing claims
help.avoid: Vague outcomes like “feel inspired”.

variables.label: Required key points
variables.placeholder: List the facts, arguments, steps, or messages that must appear.
variables.hint: Add the non-negotiable information the article must cover.

help.why: Prevents essential information from being omitted.
help.example: Only use supplied facts; Free 5/day; no fake testimonials
help.avoid: Leaving out non-negotiable claims that must appear.

variables.label: Source notes or brief
variables.placeholder: Paste approved facts, research notes, product details, or source excerpts.
variables.hint: Use this for factual material the model should rely on.

help.why: Provides a reliable factual base without creating many separate fields.
help.example: Paste product limits, verified stats, approved product stage
help.avoid: Unverified stats or private customer data.

variables.label: Output language
variables.hint: The complete package will be written in this language.

help.why: Sets the language and its natural writing conventions.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Custom language
variables.hint: Shown only when Other is selected.

help.why: Supports additional languages without expanding the primary select.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Article length
variables.hint: Auto chooses the shortest length that fully serves the topic.

help.why: Controls content depth while discouraging unnecessary output.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Custom word count
variables.hint: Use a realistic target for the available facts.

help.why: Allows precise length control only when explicitly requested.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Tone
variables.hint: Use a broad tone here; detailed voice guidance is optional below.

help.why: Provides a fast and understandable style choice.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Brand or publisher
variables.placeholder: Brand, publication, company, or author name
variables.hint: Used for positioning, attribution, and suitable CTA wording.

help.why: Anchors the article to the correct publishing identity.
help.example: Brand, publication, company, or author name
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Working title
variables.placeholder: Optional draft title or headline direction
variables.hint: The model may improve it rather than preserve it exactly.

help.why: Captures an existing editorial direction without making it mandatory.
help.example: Optional draft title or headline direction
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Reader knowledge level
variables.hint: Controls explanation depth and terminology.

help.why: Prevents the article from being too basic or too technical.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Content angle
variables.hint: Select the main way the topic should be framed.

help.why: Shapes the article’s thesis without duplicating the format field.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Point of view
variables.hint: Controls whether the article uses first, second, or third person.

help.why: Keeps pronouns and narrative voice consistent.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Brand voice guidance
variables.placeholder: Describe the voice in a few traits and examples.
variables.hint: Add preferences not covered by the general tone field.

help.why: Allows meaningful brand differentiation without a long questionnaire.
help.example: Describe the voice in a few traits and examples.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Narrative style
variables.hint: Controls how the article develops its ideas.

help.why: Changes article flow while remaining understandable to non-specialists.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Reference examples
variables.placeholder: Describe publications, articles, or styles you like.
variables.hint: Use as direction only; do not paste complete copyrighted articles.

help.why: Helps match editorial expectations without requiring direct imitation.
help.example: Describe publications, articles, or styles you like.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: What to avoid
variables.placeholder: List unwanted topics, claims, phrases, or stylistic habits.
variables.hint: Combine exclusions and prohibited phrasing in one place.

help.why: Prevents brand, editorial, and messaging conflicts.
help.example: List unwanted topics, claims, phrases, or stylistic habits.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Primary keyword
variables.hint: Leave blank for a reader-first article without a fixed keyword.

help.why: Aligns the article and metadata with a primary search query.
help.avoid: Keyword stuffing or unrelated head terms.

variables.label: Secondary keywords
variables.placeholder: Add related phrases, one per line or comma-separated.
variables.hint: Use only genuinely related terms; they will not be forced.

help.why: Supports topical coverage without adding multiple keyword fields.
help.example: Add related phrases, one per line or comma-separated.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Search intent
variables.hint: Auto infers intent from the topic, goal, and keywords.

help.why: Changes how directly the article answers and guides the reader.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Target location or market
variables.hint: Use only when location changes relevance, rules, or terminology.

help.why: Supports regional relevance without forcing geography on every article.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Internal links
variables.placeholder: Paste relevant URLs with optional anchor or page notes.
variables.hint: Only useful links will be included; no pages will be invented.

help.why: Connects the article to existing site content and conversion paths.
help.example: Paste relevant URLs with optional anchor or page notes.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: External-link policy
variables.placeholder: Choose how sources may be linked
variables.hint: The model will never invent external URLs.

help.why: Controls citation and outbound-link behavior safely.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: SEO priority
variables.hint: Reader value remains mandatory at every level.

help.why: Sets the balance between natural editorial flow and search coverage.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Snippet target
variables.hint: A snippet-friendly passage is added only when useful.

help.why: Enables a specific search-answer format without forcing one.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Content sensitivity
variables.hint: Adds stricter handling for current or high-stakes topics.

help.why: Activates appropriate caution and verification rules.
help.avoid: Treating medical/financial topics as standard marketing copy.

variables.label: Source URLs or references
variables.placeholder: Paste approved URLs or complete reference details.
variables.hint: Only supplied sources may be cited as real references.

help.why: Supports attribution and claim review without fabricated citations.
help.example: Paste approved URLs or complete reference details.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Approved quotations
variables.placeholder: Paste exact quotes with speaker and source details.
variables.hint: Quotes will be used only when attribution is sufficient.

help.why: Prevents invented quotations and unsupported attribution.
help.example: Paste exact quotes with speaker and source details.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Claims requiring support
variables.placeholder: List claims that must be evidenced, qualified, or omitted.
variables.hint: Useful for sensitive, comparative, or performance-related statements.

help.why: Creates an explicit evidence threshold for risky claims.
help.example: List claims that must be evidenced, qualified, or omitted.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Relevant jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: Country, state, province, or regulatory area
variables.hint: Used only when laws, regulations, or public rules may differ.

help.why: Prevents high-stakes content from assuming universal rules.
help.example: Country, state, province, or regulatory area
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Restrictions and disclosures
variables.placeholder: Add disclosures, required wording, brand rules, or claim limits.
variables.hint: Use one field for mandatory wording and restrictions.

help.why: Combines important compliance constraints without specialist field overload.
help.example: Add disclosures, required wording, brand rules, or claim limits.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Privacy and confidentiality
variables.placeholder: State what must be anonymized, generalized, or omitted.
variables.hint: Do not paste sensitive data unless necessary and permitted.

help.why: Protects private or confidential information in branded and case-led content.
help.example: State what must be anonymized, generalized, or omitted.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Headline options
variables.hint: Choose how many meaningfully different titles to generate.

help.why: Controls a high-value output without generating multiple full articles.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Include FAQ
variables.hint: Add only when genuine reader questions would improve the article.

help.why: Keeps FAQ content optional instead of adding it automatically.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: FAQ question count
variables.hint: Shown only when FAQ is enabled.

help.why: Controls FAQ scope without exposing unnecessary settings.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: CTA goal
variables.hint: Use one relevant CTA or choose None.

help.why: Aligns the article’s next step with the actual publishing goal.
help.avoid: Aggressive CTAs when goal is None or purely educational.

variables.label: CTA destination
variables.hint: No destination URL will be invented when this is blank.

help.why: Provides the real destination required for a usable linked CTA.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.

variables.label: Output formatting
variables.hint: Select the format easiest to paste into your publishing workflow.

help.why: Makes the result implementation-ready for different CMS workflows.
help.avoid: Invented facts, sources, quotes, guarantees, or private data.
